name: Spanakopita (Vegan)
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons water, as needed to prevent sticking
3 tablespoons Bragg liquid aminos or tamari
recipeInstructions:
Thaw one page of Phyllo dough. In a large frying pan, heat olive oil. Add the minced garlic, onion, and water; sauté until tender (8-10 minutes). Add spinach; cover and cook until wilted (2-3 minutes).
In a medium bowl, combine tofu, lemon juice, nutritional yeast flakes, raw tahini, Bragg liquid aminos or tamari, basil, thyme, and nutmeg. Stir.
Lay thawed Phyllo dough flat on a dry surface and cover with a damp towel to prevent drying out. Brush 7 sheets of filo lightly with olive oil and layer in the bottom of a lightly oiled 2 x 7 x 12-inch glass baking dish. If the dough tears, use it anyway, except for the top layer, which should not be torn for best appearance.
Spread tofu spinach filling evenly over the top of the layered dough. Fold edges of dough over the top of the filling. Brush 7 more sheets of filo lightly with olive oil and fold into the sides of the dish. Score the top layer of phyllo with a sharp knife to create serving-size pieces. This will make it easier to cut later.
Bake, uncovered, at 350°F until golden brown on top (20-30 minutes). Serve hot or cold. Triangles: To make Spanakopita into little triangles (good for appetizers or finger food for picnics and travel), lay thawed Phyllo dough flat on a dry surface. Cut sheets of dough into 3 strips (about 3½ x 12 inches) and cover with a damp towel to prevent drying out. Take two strips of dough; brush lightly with olive oil and lay on top of each other. Put 1-2 tablespoons tofu spinach filling on the bottom edge of dough. Fold dough over the filling in a triangle shape so the corner touches the opposite long side. Cut the triangle loose. Continue folding like a flag, keeping the triangle shape. Brush the top lightly with olive oil. Repeat until all the tofu spinach filling is used up. Place on a lightly oiled cookie sheet; bake at 350°F until golden brown on top (15-20 minutes).
